faqs.question: Why are crickets so common in Throgs Neck homes?
faqs.answer: Throgs Neck's waterfront location creates high humidity levels that attract crickets, while the neighborhood's many single-family homes with basements, crawl spaces, and gardens provide ideal shelter. The area's proximity to parks and green spaces along the water means crickets easily migrate into residential properties, especially during late summer and fall.
